      "それはグリコフィロウサの姿勢をしています。",
      "それはエレウサの聖母の姿勢をしています。",
      "それは聖母が幼子イエスを腕に抱いている様子を描いています。",
      "それは伝統的なギリシャ・イタリア・ビザンチン様式で彩色された衣服をしています。",
      "それは天上の領域を表す豪華なガウンの金を使用しています。",
      "それは豪華な模様と布地の折り目を反映しています。",
      "それは衣服の影がより自然で現実的な設定を作り出しています。",
      "それは聖母と幼子の顔の特徴が強調された曲線を持っています。",
      "それは複雑な影の技法を持っています。",
      "それは幼い天上の幼児が非常に巻き毛を持っていることを特徴としています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは甘いキスの聖母の姿勢を特徴としています。",
      "それは慈悲の聖母の姿勢を特徴としています。"
    ],

About: It is about the Virgin Mary and child Jesus.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Heptanese school.
It is associated with the island of Kefalonia.
It is associated with the Neo-Hellenikos Diafotismos.
It is associated with the Greek Rococo period.
It is associated with the Venetian painting style.
It is associated with the Cretan Renaissance.
It is associated with the Heptanese painting styles.
It is associated with the Aninos family.
ConsistsOf: It consists of egg tempera paint and gold leaf on a wood panel.
CreatedBy: It was created by Andreas Karadinos.
HasA: It has the glykophilousa position.
It has the eleusa Virgin position.
It has the Virgin embracing young Jesus in her arms.
It has garments colored in the traditional Greek-Italian Byzantine style.
It has gold of her sumptuous gown representative of the celestial realm.
It has luxuriant patterns and folds of fabric.
It has shadows of the clothing creating a more natural realistic setting.
It has accentuated curved facial features on the Virgin and Child.
It has a complex shadowing technique.
It has the young heavenly infant with very curly hair.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the Virgin of the sweet kiss position.
It is characterized by the Virgin of compassion position.
HeldIn: It was held in the Monastery Fanenton.
It was held in the Kimisis Church of Panagia Sami.
It was held in the Loverdos Collection.
It is maintained by the Byzantine and Christian Museum.
It is featured in the newly opened Loverdos Museum.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Venetian painting style.
IsA: It is an egg tempera painting.
It is a work of art.
It is a version of Virgin Glykofilousa.
KnownAs: It is known as the Panagia Samis.
LocatedIn: It is located in the village Komitata.
It is located on the west side of the Erissos peninsula.
It is located in the northern part of Kefalonia.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the village Komitata.
It occurred in the Monastery Fanenton.
It occurred in the church of Agios Andrea.
It occurred in the Kimisis Church of Panagia Sami.
PartOf: It is part of the iconostasis in the church of Agios Andrea.
It is part of the collection of the Kimisis Church of Panagia Sami.
It is part of the Loverdos Collection.
UsedIn: It is used in Greek-Italian Byzantine art.
It is used in the iconostasis in the church of Agios Andrea.
It is used in the collection of the Kimisis Church of Panagia Sami.
